sections.paragraph:
βολή, ῆς, ἡ (Hom. et al.; LXX) ‘act of propelling through the air by a forward motion’; in our lit. in the transf. sense distance travelled by something propelled, a throw ὡσεὶ λίθου βολήν as far as one can throw a stone, about a stone’s throw (TestGad 1:3) Lk 22:41 (Thu. 5, 65, 2 μέχρι λίθου βολῆς ἐχώρησαν; Herodian 2, 6, 13; 7, 12, 5; cp. Gen 21:16; Jos., Ant. 20, 213).—DELG s.v. βάλλω p. 162.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

transf. transf. = transferred

TestGad TestGad = Testament of Gad, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 180
